<div id="page-context-menu"
class="items-center shrink-0 min-w-[156px] justify-end ml-auto sm:flex hidden">

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#abstract"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Abstract</span>

<span data-as="p">This SEP establishes governance rules and a
standardized charter template for MCP’s two collaborative group types:
**Working Groups (WGs)** and **Interest Groups (IGs)**. Working Groups
produce concrete deliverables — SEPs, implementations, and code.
Interest Groups facilitate discussion and knowledge-sharing to identify
problems and gather requirements. The governance rules define the
requirements that all groups must follow, with lighter expectations for
IGs where appropriate. The charter template defines the structure each
group uses to document its specific mission, scope, leadership, and
work. Together they address community feedback about unclear authority
delegation and inconsistent processes across groups.</span>
<span data-as="p">This SEP is a companion to <a
href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/seps/2148-contributor-ladder.md"
class="link">SEP-2148: MCP Contributor Ladder</a>, which defines the
org-wide contributor roles (Member, Maintainer, Core Maintainer, Lead
Maintainer) referenced throughout this document. Group leadership roles
intersect with the contributor ladder: WG Leads and IG Facilitators must
hold at least Member status on the ladder, and group participation is a
recognized pathway to ladder advancement.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#motivation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Motivation</span>

<span data-as="p">Community interviews and feedback identified several
challenges with the current group structure:</span>

1.  <span data-as="p">**Unclear Authority**: It’s not always clear what
    decisions a working group can make autonomously versus what requires
    Core Maintainer approval. This leads to hesitation and
    bottlenecks.</span>
2.  <span data-as="p">**Inconsistent Decision-Making**: Different groups
    operate with different norms. Decisions made in one meeting may be
    contradicted in another, with no clear process for
    resolution.</span>
3.  <span data-as="p">**Participation Confusion**: Community members are
    uncertain about who should participate in groups, what levels of
    involvement exist, and how to become more involved.</span>
4.  <span data-as="p">**Scope Creep**: Without explicit boundaries,
    groups may gradually expand into areas owned by other groups or
    outside their mandate.</span>
5.  <span data-as="p">**Missing Escalation Paths**: When groups get
    stuck, there’s no clear path to resolution, leading to prolonged
    disagreements or abandoned initiatives.</span>
6.  <span data-as="p">**WG/IG Distinction**: The difference between
    Working Groups and Interest Groups is not always clear to
    participants, leading to mismatched expectations about outputs and
    commitment.</span>

<span data-as="p">A standardized charter template and shared governance
rules address these issues by establishing consistent processes across
all groups while requiring each group to explicitly define its specific
scope and boundaries.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#specification"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Specification</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP maintains two types of collaborative
groups:</span>

- **Working Groups (WGs)** produce concrete deliverables — SEPs,
  reference implementations, and code. Active contribution is expected.
- **Interest Groups (IGs)** facilitate discussion and knowledge-sharing
  around a topic area. They produce problem statements, use cases, and
  recommendations. Active contribution is expected.

<span data-as="p">This specification has two parts:</span>

1.  **Group Governance** — rules that apply to all MCP groups (WGs and
    IGs), with differences noted where applicable
2.  **Charter Template** — the structure each group fills in to define
    its specific mission, scope, and operations

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#part-1-group-governance"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Part 1: Group Governance</span>

<span data-as="p">The following rules apply to all MCP Working Groups
and Interest Groups. Individual charters cannot override these
requirements. Where rules differ between WGs and IGs, this is noted
explicitly.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-1-leadership"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.1 Leadership</span>

<span data-as="p">Each group has one or more **Leads** (referred to as
**Facilitators** for Interest Groups).</span>
<span data-as="p">**Requirements for all Leads and
Facilitators:**</span>

- Hold at least Member status on the <a
  href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/seps/2148-contributor-ladder.md"
  class="link">MCP Contributor Ladder</a>
- Demonstrated sustained engagement with the group’s scope area
- Ability to facilitate across organizational boundaries
- Commitment to running the group’s operations
- Group and its leadership sponsored by at least two Core Maintainers or
  one Lead Maintainer

<span data-as="p">**Additional requirements for WG Leads:**</span>

- Commitment to 2-3 hours/week for WG activities

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-2-leadership-responsibilities"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.2 Leadership Responsibilities</span>

<span data-as="p">**All Leads are responsible for:**</span>

- Schedule and facilitate regular meetings
- Set agendas in collaboration with participants and publish them in
  advance
- Ensure meeting notes are published within 48 hours
- Maintain the group’s documentation
- Maintain a members list and respective access list in
  <a href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/access" class="link"
  target="_blank"
  rel="noreferrer">https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/access</a>
- Proactively recruit and retain broad, representative membership across
  organizations and perspectives

<span data-as="p">**WG Leads are additionally responsible for:**</span>

- Drive proposals through the
  <a href="../community/sep-guidelines.html" class="link" target="_blank"
  rel="noreferrer">SEP</a> (Specification Enhancement Proposal) process
  to resolution
- Triage SEPs in the WG’s scope area, including closing SEPs that do not
  fit the roadmap (with documented rationale; authors may appeal to Core
  Maintainers)
- Escalate blocked decisions to Core Maintainers with clear context
- Maintain the working group’s roadmap
- Solicit feedback from one or more Core Maintainers on the general
  direction of the group on a continuous basis
- Provide quarterly status updates to the Community and Core Maintainer
  Group

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-3-participation-levels"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.3 Participation Levels</span>

<span data-as="p">All groups use the following participation tiers. Note
that **WG Member** is a group-specific participation level distinct from
the org-wide **Member** role defined in the <a
href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/seps/2148-contributor-ladder.md"
class="link">Contributor Ladder</a> — an individual may be a WG Member
in a specific group without holding org-wide Member status, and vice
versa.</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Level | Description | Privileges |
|----|----|----|
| **Observer** | Anyone interested in following the group’s work | Read access, may attend meetings, limited discussion participation |
| **Participant** | Active contributor to group discussions | Can propose agenda items, participate in async votes |
| **WG Member** | Sustained contributor with demonstrated expertise | Counted for quorum (WGs only) |
| **Lead/Facilitator** | Operational leadership of the group | Sets agenda, facilitates, escalates |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">Interest Groups primarily operate with Observers,
Participants, and Facilitators. IGs may adopt the WG Member tier if
their work warrants formal decision-making, but are not required
to.</span> <span data-as="p">**Becoming a WG Member (WGs, and IGs that
adopt the WG Member tier):**</span>

- Sustained participation over 3 months
- Meaningful contributions (code, spec text, reviews, or documentation)
- Nomination by existing WG Member or Lead
- No objections from Leads, Core Maintainers, or Lead Maintainers within
  7 days

<span data-as="p">**WG Member Responsibilities:**</span>

- Continue contributing in good faith
- Maintain name, organization, and Discord name in the respective
  group’s member list

<span data-as="p">**Active vs. Emeritus:** WG Members who do not
participate for 3 consecutive months are moved to emeritus status and
may return by demonstrating renewed participation.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-4-decision-making-process"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.4 Decision-Making Process</span>

<span data-as="p">This section applies primarily to Working Groups,
which make binding decisions (consensus on technical designs, spec
changes, etc.). Interest Groups typically operate by rough consensus in
discussions and do not make binding decisions — their output is
recommendations, problem statements, and use cases. IGs that adopt the
WG Member tier may use this process for internal decisions.</span>
<span data-as="p">**WG Consensus** is achieved through the following
progression. Each step is attempted before moving to the next:</span>
<span data-as="p">**Step 1: Lazy Consensus (default)**</span>

- Proposals announced with clear deadline (5 days minimum for minor
  items, 10 days for significant items)
- Silence is consent
- Any WG Member may block with documented objection
- Blocks must propose alternatives or clear criteria for resolution
- If no blocks are raised by the deadline, the proposal is accepted

<span data-as="p">**Step 2: Formal Vote (when lazy consensus is
blocked)**</span> <span data-as="p">A formal vote is triggered
when:</span>

- A WG Member blocks during the lazy consensus period
- A Lead or three or more WG Members request a formal vote

<span data-as="p">Voting rules:</span>

- Quorum: 50% of active WG Members
- Passage: Simple majority for routine matters; 2/3 majority for scope
  changes
- Core Maintainer feedback is advisory unless explicitly stated as
  binding
- All votes documented with rationale

<span data-as="p">**Step 3: Escalation (when voting does not
resolve)**</span> <span data-as="p">If a vote fails to resolve the
matter (no quorum, does not pass, or the result is contested), the Lead
escalates to Core Maintainers following the escalation path defined
below.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-5-escalation-path"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.5 Escalation Path</span>

<span data-as="p">For technical and design disagreements within a
group’s scope, groups should resolve disagreements locally before
involving Core Maintainers. For WGs, this means using the
decision-making progression (lazy consensus → vote → escalation). For
IGs, the Facilitator should attempt to find rough consensus before
escalating.</span> <span data-as="p">Some disagreements are not
appropriate for group-level resolution and should be escalated directly
to Core Maintainers:</span>

- Scope disputes (whether a topic falls within the group’s charter)
- Authority disputes (whether the group has the right to decide a
  matter)
- Cross-group conflicts (disagreements spanning multiple WGs or IGs)
- Code of conduct or behavioral concerns
- Membership or participation disputes

<span data-as="p">When escalation is necessary:</span>

1.  Lead documents the decision, options considered, and points of
    disagreement
2.  Lead presents the escalation to the Core Maintainer group with a
    clear ask
3.  The Core Maintainer group designates a CM—who should not share
    organizational affiliation with the parties involved—to resolve the
    issue and report back to the group
4.  The designated CM either: (a) provides binding guidance, (b)
    requests more information, or (c) recommends the full Core
    Maintainer group deliberate
5.  Timeline: Escalations should receive initial response within 5
    business days

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-6-meeting-requirements"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.6 Meeting Requirements</span>

<span data-as="p">Leads determine meeting frequency, format, and
duration based on the group’s current needs and lifecycle stage. There
is no fixed cadence requirement — a WG near a specification release may
meet weekly, while an IG in early exploration may meet monthly or work
primarily asynchronously.</span> <span data-as="p">Regardless of format
or frequency, all group meetings must:</span>

- Be open to all community participants (no closed or
  organization-internal meetings)
- Be published on
  <a href="https://meet.modelcontextprotocol.io" class="link"
  target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">meet.modelcontextprotocol.io</a> at
  least 7 days in advance
- Have agendas published and publicly available. The agenda or a link to
  the agenda should be published as a <a
  href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/discussions/"
  class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">GitHub Discussion in the
  Meeting Notes category</a>
- Have notes published within 48 hours to the same discussion

<span data-as="p">Leads should actively involve WG Members and
Participants in operational duties such as preparing agendas, taking
meeting notes, and facilitating discussions.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-7-communication-channels"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.7 Communication Channels</span>

<span data-as="p">All groups use the following channels:</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Channel | Purpose | Response Expectation |
|----|----|----|
| Discord `#{name}-wg` or `#{name}-ig` | Quick questions, coordination | Best effort |
| GitHub Discussions | Long-form technical discussion | Weekly triage |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">In addition to Discord, groups can establish a
discussion category in the <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/discussions/"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">GitHub Discussions</a>.
Leads will be granted the appropriate roles to manage and moderate
discussions.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-8-reporting"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.8 Reporting</span>

<span data-as="p">**Working Groups** provide quarterly updates (end of
January, April, July, October) including:</span>

- Progress against deliverables
- Blocked items and escalations
- Membership changes
- Upcoming priorities
- Resource needs

<span data-as="p">The quarterly updates are provided as a document
posted in the <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/discussions/"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">GitHub Discussions</a>
category of the Working Group. They are optionally discussed with the
Core Maintainers in a core maintainer meeting.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Interest Groups** do not have formal reporting
requirements but should keep their charter and member list
current.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-9-lifecycle"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.9 Lifecycle</span>

<span data-as="p">**Working Group Formation:**</span>

- There must be a widely acknowledged concern requiring coordination
- PR for creation of WG into `docs/community/<name>/overview.mdx`, gated
  by CODEOWNERS requiring approval by Maintainers
- PR for charter into `docs/community/<name>/charter.mdx`, gated by
  CODEOWNERS requiring approval from a single Core Maintainer (who
  should notify all Core Maintainers)
- Initial member list approved by WG Lead

<span data-as="p">**Interest Group Formation:**</span>

- Fill out the creation template in the `#wg-ig-group-creation` channel
  on
  <a href="https://discord.gg/6CSzBmMkjX" class="link" target="_blank"
  rel="noreferrer">Discord</a>
- A Core Maintainer reviews the proposal; the IG and its Facilitator(s)
  must be sponsored by at least two Core Maintainers or one Lead
  Maintainer
- Once sponsored, the Facilitator(s) organize the IG and create a
  charter

<span data-as="p">**Retirement:**</span>

- **WGs**: WG Lead or Core Maintainer proposes retirement with
  rationale; Core Maintainer or Lead Maintainer approval required. WGs
  are also retired when they have no active work for a sustained period
  or have completed all planned deliverables.
- **IGs**: Core Maintainers or Lead Maintainers may retire an IG that is
  no longer active or needed.
- In both cases, documentation is archived and channels are marked
  inactive.

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-10-charter-amendments"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1.10 Charter Amendments</span>

<span data-as="p">Changes to a group’s charter (WG or IG)
require:</span>

- Proposal by Lead/Facilitator or Core Maintainer
- Approval by Core Maintainers

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#part-2-charter-template"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Part 2: Charter Template</span>

<span data-as="p">Every MCP Working Group and Interest Group must
maintain a charter document following this template structure. Charters
are stored as MDX files at `docs/community/<group-name>/charter.mdx` in
the modelcontextprotocol repository and added to the `docs/docs.json`
file. A copyable version of this template is published at
<a href="../community/charter-template.html"
class="link"><code>docs/community/charter-template.mdx</code></a>.</span>
<span data-as="p">The charter captures information specific to each
group. Governance rules from Part 1 apply automatically and do not need
to be repeated in the charter. Sections marked **(WG only)** are
required for Working Groups but optional for Interest Groups.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#1-group-type"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">1. Group Type</span>

<span data-as="p">State whether this is a **Working Group** or an
**Interest Group**.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#2-mission-statement"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">2. Mission Statement</span>

<span data-as="p">A 2-3 sentence summary of the group’s purpose,
articulating:</span>

- The problem space being addressed
- Why cross-cutting collaboration is needed
- For WGs: what concrete deliverables the group will produce
- For IGs: what discussions and knowledge-sharing the group will
  facilitate

<span data-as="p">*WG Example:*</span>

> <span data-as="p">The Transport Working Group exists to evolve MCP’s
> transport mechanisms to support diverse deployment scenarios—from
> local subprocess communication to horizontally-scaled cloud
> deployments—while maintaining protocol coherence and backward
> compatibility.</span>

<span data-as="p">*IG Example:*</span>

> <span data-as="p">The Enterprise IG explores the challenges of
> deploying MCP in enterprise environments, gathering use cases and
> requirements to inform future specification work.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#3-scope"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">3. Scope</span>

<span data-as="p">**In Scope**: Enumerated responsibilities.</span>
<span data-as="p">For WGs, this includes:</span>

- Specification Work: Specific spec sections or SEPs owned
- Reference Implementations: SDK components or reference implementations
- Cross-Cutting Concerns: Areas requiring coordination with other groups
- Documentation: Documentation responsibilities

<span data-as="p">For IGs, this includes:</span>

- Topic areas for discussion
- Types of output (problem statements, use cases, recommendations)

<span data-as="p">**Out of Scope**: Explicit statements of what is NOT
within the group’s purview to prevent mission creep.</span>
<span data-as="p">**Related Groups**: List of other WGs or IGs with
intersecting work and nature of overlap.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#4-leadership"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">4. Leadership</span>

<span data-as="p">**Leads/Facilitators** table with:</span>

- Role, Name, Organization, GitHub handle, Term

<span data-as="p">Leadership requirements and responsibilities are
defined in the governance rules (Sections 1.1 and 1.2).</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#5-authority-&amp;-decision-rights-wg-only"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">5. Authority & Decision Rights (WG
only)</span>

<span data-as="p">Each WG must explicitly define its decision authority.
The decision-making process and escalation path are defined in the
governance rules (Sections 1.4 and 1.5). This section documents which
decisions the WG can make at which authority level.</span>
<span data-as="p">*Example:*</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Decision Type | Authority Level |
|----|----|
| Meeting logistics & scheduling | WG Leads (autonomous) |
| Proposal prioritization within WG | WG Leads (autonomous) |
| SEP triage & closure (in scope) | WG Leads (autonomous, with documented rationale) |
| Technical design within scope | WG consensus |
| Spec changes (additive) | WG consensus → Core Maintainer approval |
| Spec changes (breaking/fundamental) | WG consensus → Core Maintainer approval + wider review |
| Scope expansion | Core Maintainer approval required |
| WG Member approval | WG Member sponsors |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">IGs do not make binding decisions and do not need this
section.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#6-membership"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">6. Membership</span>

<span data-as="p">List current group members and their participation
levels, if any. Leave out if no members exist yet. Participation tiers
and membership criteria are defined in the governance rules (Section
1.3).</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#7-operations"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">7. Operations</span>

<span data-as="p">Document the group’s current meeting approach. Meeting
requirements and communication channels are defined in the governance
rules (Sections 1.6 and 1.7).</span> <span data-as="p">*Example:*</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Meeting | Frequency | Duration | Purpose |
|----|----|----|----|
| Working Session | Weekly/Biweekly | 60 min | Technical discussion, proposal review |
| Office Hours | Monthly | 30 min | Open Q&A for newcomers and observers |

</div>

</div>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#8-deliverables-&amp;-success-metrics-wg-only"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">8. Deliverables & Success Metrics (WG
only)</span>

<span data-as="p">**Active Work Items:**</span>

<div class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"
table-wrapper="true">

<div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table">

| Item          | Status                | Target Date | Champion |
|---------------|-----------------------|-------------|----------|
| SEP-XXX: Name | Draft/Review/Approved | Date        | Name     |

</div>

</div>

<span data-as="p">**Success Criteria:** Measurable outcomes for WG
success.</span> <span data-as="p">Quarterly reporting requirements are
defined in the governance rules (Section 1.8).</span>
<span data-as="p">IGs do not track formal deliverables but may list
current discussion topics or planned outputs (problem statements,
recommendations, etc.) in their charter.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#9-changelog"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">9. Changelog</span>

<span data-as="p">Track charter versions with date and changes.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#rationale"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Rationale</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#why-separate-governance-from-charter-template"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Why Separate Governance from Charter
Template?</span>

<span data-as="p">Separating fixed governance rules from the per-group
charter template makes it clear what is consistent across all groups
(decision-making, membership tiers, escalation) versus what each group
defines for itself (scope, leadership roster, deliverables). This
prevents groups from accidentally diverging on process while preserving
flexibility where it matters.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#why-cover-both-wgs-and-igs"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Why Cover Both WGs and IGs?</span>

<span data-as="p">Working Groups and Interest Groups serve different
purposes but share common operational needs — leadership, meeting
requirements, communication channels, and escalation paths. A unified
governance framework ensures consistency while clearly articulating
where IGs have lighter requirements (no formal decision authority, no
deliverables tracking, no quarterly reporting).</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#why-a-standardized-template"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Why a Standardized Template?</span>

<span data-as="p">Standardization:</span>

- Ensures all groups address critical governance questions
- Makes it easier for community members to understand any group’s
  operations
- Reduces overhead for forming new groups
- Creates accountability through explicit documentation

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#why-explicit-authority-tables"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Why Explicit Authority Tables?</span>

<span data-as="p">The authority table directly addresses the “unclear
authority” feedback. By enumerating decision types and required
approvals, WGs and community members know exactly what can be decided
autonomously versus what needs escalation. IGs are exempt from this
because they produce recommendations, not binding decisions.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#why-tiered-participation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Why Tiered Participation?</span>

<span data-as="p">Different engagement levels serve different community
needs:</span>

- **Observers** can learn without commitment
- **Participants** can contribute without full WG Member
  responsibilities
- **WG Members** take on accountability and get decision rights
  (primarily WGs)
- **Leads/Facilitators** provide operational continuity

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#why-lazy-consensus-as-default"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Why Lazy Consensus as Default?</span>

<span data-as="p">Lazy consensus:</span>

- Enables efficient decision-making for routine matters
- Reduces meeting burden
- Documents decisions through announcement/deadline structure
- Preserves blocking rights for substantive concerns

<span data-as="p">Voting is reserved for contested or high-impact
decisions.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#model-inspiration"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Model Inspiration</span>

<span data-as="p">This template is adapted from Kubernetes governance
structures and tailored for MCP’s specific needs identified through
community interviews.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#backward-compatibility"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Backward Compatibility</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#transition-for-existing-groups"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Transition for Existing Groups</span>

<span data-as="p">Working Groups and Interest Groups that exist at the
time this SEP is accepted are grandfathered in — they are recognized as
valid groups and do not need to re-apply through the formation process
defined in Section 1.9.</span> <span data-as="p">However, existing
groups must create a charter conforming to the template in Part 2 within
**8 weeks** of this SEP’s acceptance. During this transition
period:</span>

- Existing groups continue to operate under their current processes
- Leads/Facilitators are responsible for drafting the charter
- Core Maintainers will review and approve both WG and IG charters
- Groups that do not produce a charter within 8 weeks will be considered
  inactive and subject to retirement

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#security-implications"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Security Implications</span>

<span data-as="p">No direct security implications. However, clear
authority delegation and decision processes indirectly support security
by ensuring decisions are made at appropriate levels with proper
accountability.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a
href="2149-working-group-charter-template.html#reference-implementation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Reference Implementation</span>

<span data-as="p">This SEP is implemented by:</span>

1.  `docs/community/working-interest-groups.mdx` — governance rules
    (Part 1) published as the Working and Interest Groups page on
    modelcontextprotocol.io
2.  `docs/community/charter-template.mdx` — copyable charter template
    (Part 2) linked from the above
3.  Both pages added to `docs/docs.json` under the Community →
    Governance navigation group
4.  Existing WGs and IGs must create conforming charters at
    `docs/community/<name>/charter.mdx` within 8 weeks of acceptance

</div>
